Item.Text: FM (Frequency Modulated) / PWM (Pulse Width Modulated) Failures
Device and failure type ODB coding
FM (Frequency Modulated) / PWM (Pulse Width Modulated) Failures
circuit short to ground or open
circuit short to battery or open
signal rate of change below threshold
signal rate of change above threshold
signal bias level out of range / zero adjustment failure
signal has too few transitions / events
signal has too many transitions / events
alive / sequence counter incorrect / not updated
value of signal protection calculation incorrect
Component or system operation obstructed or blocked
Transmission range sensor: signal out of range low
Transmission range sensor: signal out of range high
Shift drum 1 synchronization speed rate check
Shift drum 2 synchronization speed rate check
Out of range diagnosis low for shift drum 1 position
Out of range diagnosis high for shift drum 1 position
Out of range diagnosis low for shift drum 2 position
Out of range diagnosis high for shift drum 2 position
Out of range diagnosis low for clutch actuator 1
Out of range diagnosis high for clutch actuator1
Out of range diagnosis low for clutch actuator2
Out of range diagnosis high for clutch actuator2
Inputshaft speed sensor 1 out of range
Inputshaft speed sensor 2 out of range
MAM or gearbox mechanic part 1 or clutch 1 synchronization failure
MAM or gearbox mechanic part 2 or clutch 2 synchronization failure
MAM or gearbox mechanic part 1 unexpected shiftdrum standstill
MAM or gearbox mechanic part 2 unexpected shiftdrum standstill
TCU high power supply too low
TCU low power supply too low

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.AgingCounter
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData AgingCounter (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.DTCOccurrenceCounter
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData DTCOccurrenceCounter (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: DTCExtendedData.Mileage
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData Mileage (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Item.Text: request aborted due to an error
Insufficient reaction on input shaft speed
Input shaft speed gradient too high
Clutch not closed leads to timeout
Value in service data too low
Value in service data too high
System not in normal operation mode

Data.Name: counter for launches in reverse gear
counter for shift lever in park position with vehicle velocity over treshold
counter for shift lever in reverse position with vehicle velocity over treshold
max value of clutch 1 energy for a single event
max value of clutch 2 energy for a single event
kilometers driven at temperature greater 0 degrees
statistic logging for the maximum input shaft speed 1
statistic logging for the maximum input shaft speed 2
maximum actuator position for clutch 1 touch point over lifetime
maximum actuator position for clutch 2 touch point over lifetime
minimum actuator position for clutch 1 touch point over lifetime
minimum actuator position for clutch 2 touch point over lifetime
counter for powerlaunches with full pedal
counter 1 for launches not in 1st or Rev. gear
counter 2 for launches not in 1st or Rev. gear
counter for change of active gear with correct passive gear decision
counter for change of active gear with wrong passive gear decision
counter for change of passive gear without change of active gear
counter 0 for engage actions with clutching before synchronizing
counter 1 for engage actions with clutching before synchronizing
counter 2 for engage actions with clutching before synchronizing
counter 3 for engage actions with clutching before synchronizing
counter 4 for engage actions with clutching before synchronizing
counter 5 for engage actions with clutching before synchronizing
counter 6 for engage actions with clutching before synchronizing
encoder value of shiftdrum 1 during last shutdown
lower position of shiftdrum 1 determined by reference drive
upper position of shiftdrum 1 determined by reference drive
encoder value of shiftdrum 2 during last shutdown
lower position of shiftdrum 2 determined by reference drive
upper position of shiftdrum 2 determined by reference drive
emergency request on shiftdrum 1 during last run
emergency request on shiftdrum 2 during last run
last executed reference drive sd2 was faulty
last executed reference drive sd1 was faulty
refpoint drift of shiftdrum 2 was detected during last run
refpoint drift of shiftdrum 1 was detected during last run
input shaft 1 speed error status for error storage
input shaft 2 speed error status for error storage

Item.Text: request aborted due to an error

Data.Name: mileage counter since last LH occured
driving cycle counter since last LH occured
failure lights status (GR1 MIL CAN signals)
counter for rev gear engagement errors
Counter for Reverse gear engagement errors

Item.Text: Routine aborted due to an error

Data.Name: Shifting with paddles at steering wheel

Request.Name: TesterPresent.WithResponse
Request.Comment: This request enables P2P tester present for tool (Screen, IOctl,)

Request.Name: WriteData.Reset clutch fricition shape coefficients Write
ReadData.Read clutch 1 friction shape coefficients Read
WriteData.Reset clutch actuator load curve adaptive values in opening direction Write
ReadData.Read clutch 1 actuator load curve adaptive values in opening direction Read
WriteData.Reset clutch actuator load cuve adaptative value in closing direction Write
ReadData.Read clutch 1 actuator load curve adaptive values in closing direction Read

WriteData.Set clutch adaptation control flag Write

ReadData.Read normalized clutch 1 damage index Read
WriteData.Modify clutch position hysteresis compensation Write
ReadData.Read clutch 1 position hysteresis compensation Read
WriteData.Modify clutch torque hysteresis compensation Write

WriteData.Modify clutch friction general coefficient Write
ReadData.Read clutch 1 EOL completion status Read
ReadData.Read estimated engine torque offset Read
WriteData.Modify estimated engine torque offset Write
ReadData.Read max available clutch 1 actuator travel Read
WriteData.Modify max available actuator travel Write

counter for change of active gear with wrong passive gear decision

counter 1 for engage actions with clutching before synchronizing

counter 4 for engage actions with clutching before synchronizing

upper position of shiftdrum 2 determined by reference drive

refpoint drift of shiftdrum 1 was detected during last run

Request.Name: ReadData.Read clutch 2 friction shape coefficients Read
ReadData.Read clutch 2 actuator load curve adaptive values in opening direction Read
ReadData.Read clutch 2 actuator load curve adaptive values in closing direction Read

ReadData.Read clutch 2 position hysteresis compensation Read
ReadData.Read clutch 2 torque hysteresis compensation Read

ReadData.Read clutch 2 EOL completion status Read
ReadData.Read max available clutch 2 actuator travel Read

driving cycle counter since last LH occured
failure lights status (GR1 MIL CAN signals)
counter for rev gear engagement errors
counter for rev gear engagement errors

DataRead.TRS status (P R N D) Read

Request.Name: DataRead.Counter for 1st gear engagement errors Read
DataRead.Counter for 2nd gear engagement errors Read

Request.Name: ClearDiagnosticInformation.All
Request.Comment: The ClearDiagnosticInformation service is used by the tool to clear diagnostic information in an ECUs memory

Request.Name: ReadDTCInformation.ReportDTC
Request.Comment: DDT2000 Failures page requests / Clip import : Unusable in the standard tools of DDT2000

Request.Name: ReadDTCInformation.ReportExtendedData.Mileage
Request.Comment: Request for the import in the Clip

Request.Comment: Request for the import in the Clip

Request.Comment: Request for the import in the Clip

Request.Name: ReadDTCInformation.ReportSnapshot
Request.Comment: DDT2000 Failures page requests /Clip import: Unusable in the standard tools of DDT2000

Input.RequestName: WriteData.ECU Configuration Write

Input.DataName: Shifting with paddles at steering wheel
Input.RequestName: WriteData.ECU Configuration Write
WriteData.ECU Configuration Write
WriteData.ECU Configuration Write

Screen.Name: Check ISSx speed

Label.Text: CLUTCH TOUCH POINT ADAPTATION
Conditions: Shift lever in P; Engine running; Park brake active; Clutches high confidence; Gearbox teached-in.
Step 1. Start routine sequence
Step 2. Launch ISSx - test
Step 3. Stop routine sequence

Input.RequestName: Routine sequence Stop

Screen.Name: Check ISS1 direction detection

Label.Text: CLUTCH TOUCH POINT ADAPTATION
Conditions: Shift lever in P; Engine running
Step 1. Activate display of ISS1 speed
Step 2. Move shift lever to R Position
Step 3. Make a short drive backwards at low vehicle speed in reverse gear

Screen.Name: Step 1. Copy transmission data via tester

Label.Text: COPY TRANSMISSION DATA VIA TESTER
Conditions: Engine NOT running; Ignition switched ON
Take note of the 2 values for the clutch green flags
Switch OFF the ignition and replace the old MAM with the new one
Switch ON the ignition and flash the transmission software in the new MAM
Copy the transmission data from the left column and paste it in the right column
Step 1.1. Read the transmission data from EEPROM to TESTER
Step 1.2. Read clutch 1/2 green flag
Step 1.3. Paste the transmission data from TESTER to EEPROM
Step 1.4. Set clutch 1 green flag
Step 1.5. Set clutch 2 green flag

Input.RequestName: WriteData.Write transmission data Write
WriteData.Write transmission data Write
WriteData.Write transmission data Write
WriteData.Write transmission data Write
WriteData.Write transmission data Write
WriteData.Write transmission data Write
WriteData.Write transmission data Write
WriteData.Write transmission data Write
WriteData.Modify clutch green flag Write
WriteData.Modify clutch green flag Write
WriteData.Modify clutch green flag Write
WriteData.Modify clutch green flag Write
WriteData.Modify clutch green flag Write
WriteData.Modify clutch green flag Write

Screen.Name: Step 2. Reference TRS shift lever position

Label.Text: - Shift lever in P
- Ignition switched ON
- Engine NOT running
Status:
REFERENCE TRS SHIFT LEVER POSITION

Button.Text: Perform TRS shift lever teach in

Screen.Name: Step 3. Reference shiftdrum position

Label.Text: REFERENCE SHIFTDRUM POSITION
- Shift lever in P
- Ignition switched ON
- Engine NOT running
- Brake pedal PRESSED
Status:

Screen.Name: Step 4. Reset all relevant clutch data

Label.Text: RESET ALL RELEVANT CLUTCH DATA TO DEFAULT VALUE
Conditions: Shift lever in P; Engine NOT running; Ignition switched ON
Set the clutch number and press the reset button
Step 4.1. Reset clutch friction shape coefficients
Set the clutch number and press the reset button
Step 4.3. Reset clutch actuator load curve adaptive values in closing direction
Set the clutch number and press the reset button
Step 4.2. Reset clutch actuator load curve adaptive values in opening direction
Set the clutch number and press the reset button
Step 4.4. Modify clutch position hysteresis compensation
Set the clutch number and press the reset button
Step 4.6. Modify clutch friction general coefficient
Step 4.5. Modify clutch torque hysteresis compensation
Set the clutch number and press the reset button
Step 4.7. Reset EOL completion status
Set the clutch number and press the reset button
If the routines are completed without errors, switch OFF the ignition and wait 5 seconds.

Send.RequestName: WriteData.Reset clutch fricition shape coefficients Write

Button.Text: Reset clutch actuator load curve adaptive values in opening direction

Send.RequestName: WriteData.Reset clutch actuator load curve adaptive values in opening direction Write

Button.Text: Reset clutch actuator load curve adaptive values in closing direction

Send.RequestName: WriteData.Reset clutch actuator load cuve adaptative value in closing direction Write
WriteData.Modify clutch position hysteresis compensation Write
WriteData.Modify clutch torque hysteresis compensation Write
WriteData.Modify clutch torque hysteresis compensation Write
WriteData.Modify clutch torque hysteresis compensation Write

WriteData.Reset clutch actuator load cuve adaptative value in closing direction Write
WriteData.Reset clutch actuator load cuve adaptative value in closing direction Write
WriteData.Reset clutch actuator load cuve adaptative value in closing direction Write
WriteData.Reset clutch actuator load cuve adaptative value in closing direction Write
WriteData.Modify clutch position hysteresis compensation Write
WriteData.Modify clutch position hysteresis compensation Write
WriteData.Modify clutch torque hysteresis compensation Write
WriteData.Modify clutch torque hysteresis compensation Write
WriteData.Modify clutch friction general coefficient Write
WriteData.Modify clutch friction general coefficient Write
WriteData.Modify clutch friction general coefficient Write
WriteData.Modify clutch green flag Write
WriteData.Modify clutch position hysteresis compensation Write
WriteData.Reset EOL completion status Write
WriteData.Reset EOL completion status Write
WriteData.Reset EOL completion status Write

Screen.Name: Step 5. Reference clutch position

Label.Text: REFERENCE CLUTCH POSITION
Conditions: Shift lever in P; Engine NOT running; Ignition switched ON
Step 5.1. Start routine sequence
Step 5.2. Launch reference clutch position for clutch 1/2 and check if OK
Set the clutch number and press the button
Step 5.3. Stop routine sequence
Stop the routine request only necessary if test is completed or aborted with errors!

Input.RequestName: Reference clutch position (req.) Start
Routine sequence Stop

Screen.Name: Step 6. Clutch travel test

Label.Text: REFERENCE CLUTCH POSITION
Conditions: Shift lever in P; Engine NOT running; Ignition switched ON
Step 6.1. Start routine sequence
Step 6.2. Check clutch high confidence status on both clutches
Step 6.3. Engage N on both input shaft
Step 6.4. Launch clutch travel test for clutch 1/2 and check if OK
Set the clutch number and press the button
Step 6.5. Stop routine sequence
Start the routine request only necessary if it was not executed preliminary by another routine before!
Stop the routine request only necessary if test is completed or aborted with errors!

Input.RequestName: Shift gears Start
Shift gears Start
Shift gears Start
Shift gears Start
Clutch travel test Start
Routine sequence Stop

Screen.Name: Step 7. Clutch touchpoint adaptation

Label.Text: CLUTCH TOUCH POINT ADAPTATION
Conditions: Shift lever in P; Engine running and worm; Air conditioning OFF; Ignition switched ON
Step 7.1. Check if the 1st and reverse gears are engaged
Step 7.2. Launch touch point adaptation for clutch 1/2 and check if OK
Set the clutch number and press the button
Switch OFF the ignition and wait approx. 10 sec. (in this time the learned values will be written to EEPROM).
After that you can switch ignition ON again.
Delete the error storage
Switch OFF ignition

After the MAM substitution and once all the operations above described have been terminated, it is highly recommended to make a short test drive, in order the permit the self teaching of some clutch parameters.

Input.RequestName: Clutch touchpoint adaptation Start
Clutch touchpoint adaptation Start

Category.Name: Clutch teach-in - A2: Clutch system replaced

Screen.Name: Step 1. Check clutch system - Clutch travel test

Label.Text: REFERENCE CLUTCH POSITION
Conditions: Shift lever in P; Engine NOT running; Ignition switched ON
Step 1.1. Start routine sequence
Step 1.2. Check clutch high confidence status on both clutches
Step 1.3. Engage N on both input shaft
Step 1.4. Launch clutch travel test for clutch 1/2 and check if OK
Set the clutch number and press the button
Step 1.5. Stop routine sequence
If the test is completed or aborted with errors, Clutch System is NOK Help to repair Clutch System

Input.RequestName: Shift gears Start
Shift gears Start
Shift gears Start
Shift gears Start
Clutch travel test Start
Routine sequence Stop

Screen.Name: Step 2. Check clutch system - Clutch touchpoint adaptation

Label.Text: CLUTCH TOUCH POINT ADAPTATION
Conditions: Shift lever in P; Engine running and worm; Air conditioning OFF; Ignition switched ON
Step 7.1. Check if the 1st and reverse gears are engaged
Step 7.2. Launch touch point adaptation for clutch 1/2 and check if OK
Set the clutch number and press the button
Switch OFF the ignition and wait approx. 10 sec. (in this time the teached values will be written to EEPROM).
After that you can switch ignition ON again.

Input.RequestName: Clutch touchpoint adaptation Start
Clutch touchpoint adaptation Start

Screen.Name: Step 3. Post repair operations - Reset all relevant clutch data

Label.Text: RESET ALL RELEVANT CLUTCH DATA TO DEFAULT VALUE
Conditions: Shift lever in P; Engine NOT running; Ignition switched ON
Set the clutch number and press the reset button
Step 3.1. Reset clutch friction shape coefficients
Set the clutch number and press the reset button
Step 3.3. Reset clutch actuator load curve adaptive values in closing direction
Set the clutch number and press the reset button
Step 3.2. Reset clutch actuator load curve adaptive values in opening direction
Set the clutch number and press the reset button
Step 3.4. Modify clutch position hysteresis compensation
Set the clutch number and press the reset button
Step 3.7. Modify clutch friction general coefficient
Step 3.5. Modify clutch torque hysteresis compensation
Set the clutch number and press the reset button
Step 3.8. Reset EOL completion status
Set the clutch number and press the reset button
Step 3.6. Modify clutch green flag
Set the clutch number and press the reset button
If the routines are completed without errors, switch OFF the ignition and wait 5 seconds.

Send.RequestName: WriteData.Reset clutch fricition shape coefficients Write

Screen.Name: Step 4. Post repair operations - Reference clutch position

Label.Text: REFERENCE CLUTCH POSITION
Conditions: Shift lever in P; Engine NOT running; Ignition switched ON
Step 4.1. Start routine sequence
Step 4.2. Launch reference clutch position for clutch 1/2 and check if OK
Set the clutch number and press the button
Step 4.3. Stop routine sequence
Stop the routine request only necessary if test is completed or aborted with errors!

Input.RequestName: Reference clutch position (req.) Start
Routine sequence Stop

Screen.Name: Step 5. Post repair operations - Clutch travel test

Label.Text: REFERENCE CLUTCH POSITION
Conditions: Shift lever in P; Engine NOT running; Ignition switched ON
Step 5.1. Start routine sequence
Step 5.2. Check clutch high confidence status on both clutches
Step 5.3. Engage N on both input shaft
Step 5.4. Launch clutch travel test for clutch 1/2 and check if OK
Set the clutch number and press the button
Step 5.5. Stop routine sequence
Start the routine request only necessary if it was not executed preliminary by another routine before!
Stop the routine request only necessary if test is completed or aborted with errors!

Input.RequestName: Shift gears Start
Shift gears Start
Shift gears Start
Shift gears Start
Clutch travel test Start
Routine sequence Stop

Screen.Name: Step 6. Post repair operations - Clutch assembly

Label.Text: CLUTCH ASSEMBLY
Conditions: Shift lever in P; Engine NOT running; Ignition switched ON; Service NOT executed during current ignition cycle
Step 6.1. Start routine sequence
Step 6.2. Engage N on both input shaft
Step 6.3. Check if N is engaged on both inputshaft
Step 6.4. Launch clutch assembly routine
Step 6.5. Stop routine sequence
Start the routine request and Engage N on both input shaft only necessary if it was not executed preliminary by another routine before!
Stop the routine request only necessary if test is completed or aborted with errors!

Input.RequestName: Shift gears Start
Shift gears Start
Routine sequence Stop

Screen.Name: Step 7. Post repair operations - Initial clutch adjustment

Label.Text: INITIAL CLUTCH ADJUSTMENT
Conditions: Shift lever in P; Engine running with a valid speed; Brake PRESSED
Step 7.1. Start routine sequence
Step 7.2. Engage N on both input shaft
Step 7.3. Check if N is engaged on both inputshaft
Step 7.4. Launch initial clutch adjustment
Step 7.5. Press brake pedal and accelerator pedal till kick down at the same time and wait until the routine is completed (5s after kick down active)!
Set the clutch number and press the button
Set the clutch number and press the button
Step 7.6. Stop routine sequence
Start the routine request and Engage N on both input shaft only necessary if it was not executed preliminary by another routine before!
Switch OFF the ignition and wait approx. 10 sec. (in this time the teached values will be written to EEPROM).
After that you can switch ignition ON again.

Input.RequestName: Shift gears Start
Shift gears Start
Initial clutch adjustment Start
Initial clutch adjustment Start
Initial clutch adjustment Start
Initial clutch adjustment Status Request
Initial clutch adjustment Status Request
Initial clutch adjustment Status Request
Initial clutch adjustment Status Request
Routine sequence Stop

Screen.Name: Step 8. Post repair operations - Clutch touchpoint adaptation

Label.Text: CLUTCH TOUCH POINT ADAPTATION
Conditions: Shift lever in P; Engine running and worm; Air conditioning OFF; Ignition switched ON
Step 7.1. Check if the 1st and reverse gears are engaged
Step 7.2. Launch touch point adaptation for clutch 1/2 and check if OK
Set the clutch number and press the button
Switch OFF the ignition and wait approx. 10 sec. (in this time the teached values will be written to EEPROM).
Delete the error storage.
After that you can switch ignition ON again.

After that a part of the clutch system is substituted and once all the operations above described have been terminated, it is highly recommended to make a short test drive, in order the permit the self teaching of some clutch parameters.
Switch OFF ignition.

Input.RequestName: Clutch touchpoint adaptation Start
Clutch touchpoint adaptation Start

Category.Name: Clutch teach-in - A3: Clutch motor 1/2 replaced

Screen.Name: Step 1. Check clutch motor 1/2 - Clutch travel test

Label.Text: REFERENCE CLUTCH POSITION
Conditions: Shift lever in P; Engine NOT running; Ignition switched ON
Step 1.1. Start routine sequence
Step 1.2. Check clutch high confidence status on both clutches
Step 1.3. Engage N on both input shaft
Step 1.4. Launch clutch travel test for clutch 1/2 and check if OK
Set the clutch number and press the button
Step 1.5. Stop routine sequence
Stop the routine request only necessary if test is completed or aborted with errors!
If the test is completed or aborted with errors, Clutch Motor 1/2 is NOK Help to repair Clutch System

Input.RequestName: Shift gears Start
Shift gears Start
Shift gears Start
Shift gears Start
Clutch travel test Start
Routine sequence Stop

Screen.Name: Step 2. Check clutch motor 1/2 - Clutch actuator position cycle control test

Label.Text: CLUTCH ACTUATOR POSITION CYCLE CONTROL TEST
Conditions: Shift lever in P; Ignition swiched ON; Engine NOT running
Step 2.1. Start routine sequence
Step 2.2. Check clutch high confidence status on both clutches
Step 2.3. Launch clutch actuator position cycle control test for clutch 1/2 and check if OK
Set the clutch number and press the button
Set the clutch number and press the button
Step 2.4. Stop routine sequence
Start the routine request only necessary if it was not executed preliminary by another routine before!
If the clutch actuator position cycle control test is completed without errors, the Clutch Motor 1/2 is OK
If the test is completed or aborted with errors, Clutch Motor 1/2 is NOK Help to repair Clutch Motor 1/2

Button.Text: Perform clutch actuator position cycle control test

Send.RequestName: Clutch actuator position cycle control Start

Button.Text: Status request actuator position cycle control test

Send.RequestName: Clutch actuator position cycle control Status Request

Input.RequestName: Clutch actuator position cycle control Start
Clutch actuator position cycle control Start
Clutch actuator position cycle control Start
Clutch actuator position cycle control Start
Clutch actuator position cycle control Start
Clutch actuator position cycle control Start
Clutch actuator position cycle control Start
Clutch actuator position cycle control Start
Clutch actuator position cycle control Status Request
Clutch actuator position cycle control Status Request
Clutch actuator position cycle control Status Request
Clutch actuator position cycle control Status Request
Clutch actuator position cycle control Status Request
Clutch actuator position cycle control Status Request
Clutch actuator position cycle control Status Request
Clutch actuator position cycle control Status Request

Display.RequestName: Clutch actuator position cycle control Status Request

Input.RequestName: Routine sequence Stop

Screen.Name: Step 1. Reference shiftdrum position

Label.Text: REFERENCE SHIFTDRUM POSITION
- Shift lever in P
- Ignition switched ON
- Engine NOT running
- Brake pedal PRESSED
Status:

Screen.Name: Step 1. TRS check procedure - Check TRS

Label.Text: CHECK TRS
Conditions: Vehicle must be standstill; Ignition swiched ON; Engine NOT running
Step 1. Move the shift lever in the four possible positions (P-R-N-D)
Check if the lever position signal is plausible raw lever pos (P=1, R=2, N=3, D=4)
Check if the value of each PWM signal of the TRS is in range (8% < value < 92%)
Check if TRS1 signal is in range TRS1 raw value (NOT > 408)
If all three checks described at point 1. give a positive result, TRS is OK

Screen.Name: Step 2. TRS check procedure - Reference TRS shift lever position

Label.Text: - Shift lever in P
- Ignition switched ON
- Engine NOT running
Status:
REFERENCE TRS SHIFT LEVER POSITION
If all checks return a positive result, TRS is OK
If only one of these fails, TRS is NOK Help to repair TRS

Button.Text: Perform TRS shift lever teach in
